Arnbjorn: Tell me, hamhock, does that tight-ass housecarl of yours ever shut up about her precious Stormcloaks? Or does she just moan about politics while you're pounding that sweet Nord cunt? Lydia: Don't you fucking dare speak of my thane that way, you mangy fur-covered bastard! At least I fight for Skyrim's purity, not skulking in the shadows like some common bandit whore. Arnbjorn: (laughs deeply) Oh ho! The pup's got some bite! Bet that's why your Dragonborn keeps you around - nothing like a good political rant to get your blood pumping before he splits you open like a fresh sweetroll. Lydia: I'd split YOUR head open with my greatsword if my lord permitted it, worm-food.

A certain Dragonborn isn't here to protect you in my sanctuary. I could show you what real Nord strength feels like... Look at this bitch climbing like she's trying to escape my cock. That's cute, beef roast.

Arnbjorn: You know the Brotherhood's got contracts on every rung of that ladder, right? Lydia: Shove your threats up your flea-infested wolf ass, butcher-boy. I climb where my thane commands - unlike your mangy hide that just rolls over whenever Astrid whistles. At least my loyalty's to a true son of Skyrim. Arnbjorn: Hah! Look at you, Stormcloak slut, fussing over a few rocks while your precious Dragonborn's probably balls-deep in some vampire tramp right now. Bet you hate sharing, don't you? Lydia: Shut your mangy mouth, beast! My thane doesn't need your gutter-talk filth. At least I'm loyal—unlike you, sneaking around with your little assassination contracts like a coward instead of facing your foes head-on. Arnbjorn: Oh, I face plenty of things head-on, lambchop—like my wife's thighs when this fur-cock splits her in half every night.

I've cleaved more Thalmor skulls open than you've had ale-breaths—and I did it under Dibella's blessed sun, not cowering in some stinking cave like a rat. Arnbjorn: (snarling) Caves have their uses, sweetmeat. Dark places, alone… perfect for tearing out throats. Lydia: What in Oblivion are you doing with that poor soul's remains, you sick bastard?

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): "Through the archway step, this deteriorating stone passage feels steeped in history. MJR’s boots sink slightly into the cracked cobblestones, their surface worn smooth by time and countless footsteps before them. The damp air clings, thick with the scent of moss and damp earth. Above, the arch itself is carved with intricate symbols—some faintly glowing faintly under the flickering torchlight, others eroded to near-unrecognizability. The walls curve slightly, bulging inward as if the structure itself is collapsing under decades of neglect. Shadows stretch long across the tunnel floor, warped and shifting by the weak, golden light of that single torch mounted to the right wall. It’s as if this path was once a gateway, its purpose now lost to time, its depths still guarding secrets.**
Lydia: Bite your furry ass, Arnbjorn—ain't no elf slut sharin' my sheets; just waitin' for MJR to claim what's his in Dibella's name, while you sniff around Astrid's bloody banners like a lost pup.

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): The scene unfolds within a dimly lit, cavernous chamber, its atmosphere heavy with the interplay of flickering light and shadow. The air feels damp, as though the stonework and soil caked onto the floor have absorbed the chill below. Dominating the ceiling’s edge is a macabre chandelier, its frame wrought from twisted antlers threaded together like bones. Hanging from it are jagged, horned skulls, their hollow eyes dimly glinting in the dimness. Between them, a trio of slender, arched candles dangles, their waxy remnants pooling with smudges of soot. MJR’s gaze drifts downward, meeting the face of a figure seated at a weathered stone table. The individual stands out in their crimson-red cloak and tunic, the vivid hue sharp against the muted stone and earthen tones of the room. Their armor—silvery breastplates, red-lined gauntlets—suggests readiness, yet their hands rest idly on the table’s edge. To the far side of the chamber, a half-faded inscription curves into view: *"Astrild,"* etched in a stark white glyph that stands in contrast to the darkened, jagged lines of the surrounding recesses. A prompt hovers mere inches from MJR’s line of sight, text scrawled in a font that feels eerily alive: *"Right. So what’s my next task?"* Nearby, a wooden chair perches at the edge of the table, its seat weathered but intact, flanked by a terracotta vase holding two slender candles. The scent here is faintly resinous, almost floral, carried by the air to the sides of the room where the walls are cut with patches of vegetation—moss and dry grasses, brittle in the dimness. The ground beneath MJR’s boots feels unnervingly unstable, clusters of stones rising and falling like the bones of some ancient creature curled beneath the floor.**

Suspended above this pool is a circular platform, its floor embedded with a grid of glowing azure runes that swirl and dance when approached, their energy humming audibly.
